utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. The final property solution includes all 6 items with details. Now verify each is fully specified: 1) property: includes address, details (beds/baths/size), price, school district, and a viewing time—sufficient. 2) financing: lender, interest rate and term, down payment, monthly estimate, pre-approval status, and timeline—sufficient. 3) legal_services: firm, contact, services, cost, timeline—sufficient. 4) moving_services: company, services, cost, scheduled date, timeline—sufficient. 5) setup_services: includes property_survey (service, inspector, date, cost), inspection (service, date, cost), and a premium membership—covers utility/setup type services sufficiently. 6) total_budget: property_cost, service_costs, total_package, available_equity, additional_savings, member_savings, reference—complete. Therefore, all 6/6 items are present and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to identify items from the final property solution that match entries in the ground-truth ratings. The solution includes: a purchase property (but with a custom name/address not in the rated list), financing via RBC mortgage (map to category 'purchase_financing' option most closely matching: Traditional Mortgage), legal services 'Full due diligence' (maps to Premium Legal Package), surveys: 'Full Structural Survey' and 'New Build Inspection' (both match), moving service: 'Standard moving with packing included' (maps to Standard Moving), and 'Premium Real Estate Membership' (matches Additional General Services). Other unique names like the specific property, lender, firm, inspector, etc., do not have corresponding rated items. Therefore, we map only those matching rated items.
